steps.explanation: The original map side is 30 inches, and 1 inch represents 17 miles. The full 30-inch side represents 30 \times 17 = 510 actual miles.
The smaller map is 65% shorter, so it keeps only 35% of the original side length: 30 \times 0.35 = 10.5 inches. That same 510 actual miles is now compressed into 10.5 inches.
The new scale is 510 \div 10.5 \approx 48.57 miles per inch.

• A: 5.95 — This is less than the original 17 miles per inch, but a shorter map must represent more miles per inch, not fewer. Eliminated.
  • B: 10.30 — Also less than 17; same reasoning rules this out. Eliminated.
  • C: 19.50 — Larger than 17 but far smaller than 510 ÷ 10.5. Eliminated.
  • D: 48.57 — 510 ÷ 10.5 ≈ 48.57. That matches choice D — D is correct.
